sanskrit:
यतो यतो निचलति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्।
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत्॥
transliteration:
yato yato niścalati manaś cañcalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmany eva vaśaṃ nayet
literal_meaning: From wherever the unsteady mind wanders, one should draw it back and bring it under the control of the Self.
contextual_meaning: A practical instruction for attention-training: notice the mind’s drift, label it, and gently redirect attention to the chosen anchor (Self).
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive awareness: observing the wandering mind without judgment and returning attention to an internal locus of control.
trigger_scenario: You’re doom-scrolling at 2 a.m.; you notice the compulsion, close the app, and bring attention to your breath.
anchor_text: Notice the drift, name it, and gently return attention to the calm Self within.
